headerSections.textBlock:
This raid was reprised in the Age of Triumph update. In addition to updated weapons and armor, it also features additional new rewards and can be the Featured Raid for the week.

A featured raid has multiple bonuses involved. The first clear account-wide will provide an Age of Triumph Ornament. All Challenges in the Raid are active, and completion of them awards further Age of Triumph Ornaments as well as 1 or 2 Exotic Adept versions of raid primary weapons. Age of Triumph Ornaments can be used to unlock alternate appearances on Year 3 raid armor pieces.

activityCards.title: Vault of Glass
activityCards.subtitle: Ishtar Sink, Venus
activityCards.description: The original Year 1 Normal mode raid.

activityCards.title: Vault of Glass (Heroic)
activityCards.subtitle: Ishtar Sink, Venus
activityCards.description: The Year 1 Hard mode raid.

activityCards.title: Vault of Glass (Epic)
activityCards.subtitle: Ishtar Sink, Venus
activityCards.description: An unreleased version of the Year 1 raid, labeled as tier 3 with the Epic modifier applied. Potentially the intended source for the unfinished alternate raid armor set.

crotas-end-reprised.headline: Crota's End
crotas-end-reprised.flavorText: He waits in the dark below.

headerSections.textBlock:
This raid was reprised in the Age of Triumph update. In addition to updated weapons and armor, it also features additional new rewards and can be the Featured Raid for the week.

A featured raid has multiple bonuses involved. The first clear account-wide will provide an Age of Triumph Ornament. All Challenges in the Raid are active, and completion of them awards further Age of Triumph Ornaments as well as 1 or 2 Exotic Adept versions of raid primary weapons. Age of Triumph Ornaments can be used to unlock alternate appearances on Year 3 raid armor pieces.

activityCards.title: Crota's End
activityCards.subtitle: Ocean of Storms, Moon
activityCards.description: The Year 1 Normal mode raid.

activityCards.title: Crota's End (Heroic)
activityCards.subtitle: Ocean of Storms, Moon
activityCards.description: The Year 1 Hard mode raid.

headerSections.textBlock:
This raid was reprised in the Age of Triumph update. In addition to updated weapons and armor, it also features additional new rewards and can be the Featured Raid for the week.

A featured raid has multiple bonuses involved. The first clear account-wide will provide an Age of Triumph Ornament. All Challenges in the Raid are active, and completion of them awards further Age of Triumph Ornaments as well as 1 or 2 Exotic Adept versions of raid primary weapons. Age of Triumph Ornaments can be used to unlock alternate appearances on Year 3 Harrowed armor pieces.

activityCards.title: King's Fall
activityCards.subtitle: Dreadnaught, Rings of Saturn
activityCards.description: The Year 2 Normal mode raid.

activityCards.title: King's Fall (Heroic)
activityCards.subtitle: Dreadnaught, Rings of Saturn
activityCards.description: The Year 2 Hard mode raid.

wrath-of-the-machine-featured.headline: Wrath of the Machine
wrath-of-the-machine-featured.flavorText: Where the Light ends, evolution begins.

headerSections.textBlock:
The 390 Light version is only available when Featured for the week. Only this Featured version provides Challenges, but all other rewards are the same between 390 Featured and 380 Heroic.

A featured raid has multiple bonuses involved. The first clear account-wide will provide an Age of Triumph Ornament. All Challenges in the Raid are active, and completion of them awards further Age of Triumph Ornaments as well as 1 or 2 Exotic Adept versions of raid primary weapons. Age of Triumph Ornaments can be used on Spliced armor to unlock alternate visuals.

Wrath of the Machine has a SIVA Cache chest system which allows you to spend 1 SIVA Cache Key (created from 10 SIVA Key Fragments) to gain an additional drop from each encounter's rewards once per character per week. Normal and Heroic chests are present, so be aware of what you are opening.

activityCards.title: Wrath of the Machine
activityCards.subtitle: Old Russia, Earth
activityCards.description: The Normal mode raid.

activityCards.title: Wrath of the Machine
activityCards.subtitle: Old Russia, Earth
activityCards.description: The Heroic mode raid.

headerSections.textBlock:
Each week at reset, a random Strike among the SIVA Crisis playlist options will be selected as the Weekly Nightfall Strike. The Nightfall has increased difficulty compared to other Strikes, and does not feature matchmaking. Every 4th week the beneficial Daybreak modifier will be included.

The Nightfall does not feature a kick-to-orbit mechanic, despite it’s prior inclusion in Destiny 1 and later inclusion in Destiny 2. A full team wipe (or death while solo) will only reset back to the last checkpoint attained, usually the beginning of the current encounter. Nightfall completion provides valuable rewards that can only be claimed once per week, and bestows the **Radiant Light** buff until weekly reset which increases experience and reputation gains by 25% (multiplicative with other increases).

summaryRewards.rewardText: (Guaranteed 1 of) Legendary Item | Exotic | 17-20 Strange Coins | 10-12 Three of Coins
(Possible) Sparrow Toolkit or 2015 Winter Emote Box

activityCards.title: Sepiks Perfected
activityCards.description: The Fallen House of Devils has found a way to resurrect Sepiks, their exalted Servitor and one time God. Fight to the Devils' Lair and destroy him.

activityCards.title: The Abomination Heist
activityCards.description: Fallen Devil Splicers are intent on using SIVA technology to experiment on the Hive. Stop them from stealing an abomination from the Summoning Pits.

activityCards.title: The Wretched Eye
activityCards.description: A Fallen high priest is warping an ancient science to his own twisted ends. Fight to the heart of a Hive nest to find the priest and end his atrocities.

activityCards.title: The Nexus
activityCards.description: The Vex world-eating machine below the Academy has reawakened. Descend and dismantle the Mind that controls it.

activityCards.title: The Shadow Thief
activityCards.description: The Devil Splicers made Taniks Perfected. He's back in his old Wolfship near the Moon. Board it and eliminate the threat he poses.

activityCards.title: Fallen S.A.B.E.R.
activityCards.description: Rush to the aid of the AI Warmind Rasputin as he broadcasts a plea for help across the system.

activityCards.title: Shield Brothers
activityCards.description: Travel deep into the heart of the Dreadnaught in pursuit of a Cabal assault team. Stop it from detonating the Hive ship's core.

activityCards.title: The Sunless Cell
activityCards.description: Assault a prison deep in the heart of the Dreadnaught and put an end to a Hive champion.

activityCards.title: Echo Chamber
activityCards.description: Guardians destroyed the Nexus on Venus. Now, a powerful Vex mind seeks to sunder time and space and undo what was done.

activityCards.title: Blighted Chalice
activityCards.description: A blight of Taken now roosts In the Hellmouth. Unchecked, they could refocus the Darkness within and descend on the City.

activityCards.title: Winter’s Run
activityCards.description: As the Vex march to claim the Ishtar Sink, the Fallen House of Winter is raising a powerful Archon, stolen from the Prison of Elders in the Reef. Find the Archon before they fully restore his soul.

activityCards.title: Cerberus Vae III
activityCards.description: Just beyond their warbase, Valus Ta'aurc, Fleet Commander of the Cabal Siege Dancers, hides in an Imperial Land Tank. Fight through his guard and stop this terror before he destroys all of Freehold.

activityCards.title: Dust Palace
activityCards.description: A new Cabal force has unleashed powerful Psion Flayers to seize control of the old Martian Warmind. Face this new threat and secure what remains of the ancient network.

activityCards.title: The Undying Mind
activityCards.description: An ancient Mind, feared by the Vex themselves, hides among the channels of the Black Garden. Find this machine and end its existence.

activityCards.title: The Will of Crota
activityCards.description: Omnigul, the general of Crota's armies, has been reborn. Eliminate her, along with the Hive that brought her back.

sections.textBlock: End screen Legendary gear rewards consist of Year 3 Vanguard, Crucible, and Faction weapons and armor, as well as the Vanguard Shell. Faction gear may be more likely and preferential toward your pledged Faction but data is too limited to confirm.

headerSections.textBlock:
Playlist of Year 3 Strikes, containing versions of all available Strikes. All Strikes included have a Strike Hoard chest, and the potential for the boss to drop a Skeleton Key. These Strikes also have modifiers added to increase difficulty which change at weekly reset.

Additional rewards on the post-mission Activity Rewards screen include possible Motes of Light or Strange Coins. If any further rewards are possible from this screen, they have not been sufficiently documented.

All strike playlists feature a Vanguard Streak mechanic, as long as you do not leave matchmaking for the playlist in any way. The streak may also break when matchmade into an in-progress Strike. The bonus begins after your first Strike completion. Despite the in-game description, the Strike Streak bonus does not appear to increase Vanguard Reputation gains for this playlist, and only improves the quality and/or quantity of engram drops from bosses.

summaryRewards.rewardText: Legendary Marks +10 (Account Weekly Limit: 3 | Shared with Prison of Elders: Taken)
Experience +1,820 (May Vary by Strike or Score)

headerSections.textBlock:
Playlist of Year 3 Strikes, containing versions of all available Strikes. All Strikes included have a Strike Hoard chest, and a lower potential for the boss to drop a Skeleton Key. The primary reward focus for this activity are the Rare Engram drops from boss kills, which assist in leveling up their decryption cap of Light Level 365.

Additional rewards on the post-mission Activity Rewards screen include possible Motes of Light or Strange Coins. If any further rewards are possible from this screen, they have not been sufficiently documented.

All strike playlists feature a Vanguard Streak mechanic, as long as you do not leave matchmaking for the playlist in any way. The streak may also break when matchmade into an in-progress Strike. The bonus begins after your first Strike completion. Despite the in-game description, the Strike Streak bonus does not appear to increase Vanguard Reputation gains for this playlist, and only improves the quality and/or quantity of engram drops from bosses.

Rewards appear to be identical to the base difficulty versions of the same Strikes selectable from the map, but as a continuous playlist.

Additional rewards on the post-mission Activity Rewards screen include possible Motes of Light or Strange Coins. If any further rewards are possible from this screen, they have not been sufficiently documented.

The bonus begins after your first Strike completion. activityCards.title: The Devils’ Lair
activityCards.description: The Fallen House of Devils in the Cosmodrome sustains its strength through a single Servitor known as Sepiks Prime. Find where this keeper of souls lies and destroy him.

activityCards.title: The Summoning Pits
activityCards.description: Deep within the Hellmouth, the Hive are breeding powerful abominations to unleash upon Earth. Find the pit where they live and stop any new horrors from rising.

activityCards.title: The Nexus
activityCards.description: The Golden Age legends tell of ancient tunnels below the Academy, where a Vex machine once ate away at the planet. Descend into the old dig sites and find this Nexus before it churns again.

headerSections.textBlock:
Challenge of the Elders is a special variation of Prison of Elders, featuring 3 rounds of mini-boss fights, with 2 modifiers applied, and a specified method of killing enemies awarding bonus points. Bosses, modifiers, and score bonus change at weekly reset. To participate in the Challenge of Elders, you must first acquire an Elders’ Sigil from Variks for 100 Glimmer. This will be your scorecard for the week, tracking your progress toward your two objectives: a single-run high score and cumulative run total score.

Five minutes after announcing each round Variks will begin deducting points from your score, so find a balance between killing for score and defeating the round boss. Unlike Prison of Elders, the activity ends as soon as the final round is completed with no treasure room.

**NOTE:** There are two versions of Challenge of the Elders. The version in the Reef Destination screen is the **320** version. The version in the Featured section of the main Director screen is the **390** version. They have the same name, and use the same weekly settings, but you cannot toggle between the two via the difficulty selector. Be aware of what version you are running; only the 390 version has end screen rewards, and reportedly the 320 version can only reward your through the Elders’ Sigil.

sections.textBlock: Rewards from the Elders’ Sigil are obtained by achieving the two score objectives for the week. Reaching a high score of 30,000 or more in a single run will award you an Elders’ Weapon package, and reaching a cumulative score of 90,000 or more across as many runs as you require will award you an Elders’ Armor package. Both rewards are limited to once per Guardian per week, and must be claimed at Variks.
Bosses in Challenge of the Elders are the final round bosses from Prison of Elders: Taken, however each has been given additional abilities or modified to increase the difficulty.

subjectDetailCards.title: Pilot Servitor
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Fallen
subjectDetailCards.description: Teleports to various locations in the arena, and will release waves of bouncing shock grenades to attack the area all around it.

subjectDetailCards.title: Wretched Knight
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Hive
subjectDetailCards.description: Can summon Taken Defensive Wards around the arena that serve as traps, detonating if a Guardian get too close.

subjectDetailCards.title: Overmind Minotaur
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Vex
subjectDetailCards.description: Will attempt to lock its sights onto Guardians, and then trap them in a Detainment Field. Break line of sight before this ability activates to avoid being caught. Detainment fields prevent movement but not turning or attacking, so if caught destroy the field quickly.

subjectDetailCards.title: Val Aru’un
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Cabal
subjectDetailCards.description: Accompanied by Psion Flayers, who will begin to channel a protective shield onto the boss. Defeat the elemental matching Psion Flayer to temporarily drop the shield. Psion Flayers are not restored by reinforcements, so when all 3 are dead the boss will remain vulnerable.

subjectDetailCards.title: Keksis, the Betrayed
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Taken Fallen
subjectDetailCards.description: Will summon a Taken mine that will suppress all Guardians in the arena if detonated. Stand in the radius of the mine to channel its Corrupted Light and defuse the bomb.

subjectDetailCards.title: Sylok, the Defiled
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Taken Hive
subjectDetailCards.description: Taken Burn solar projectile ability fires in larger amounts with higher damage, and leaves behind fire in a wider area for a longer amount of time.

subjectDetailCards.title: Seditious Mind
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Taken Vex
subjectDetailCards.description: Will periodically target a random Guardian with an explosive Blight attack. The targeted area is indicated with a red ring on the ground, and detonates within a few seconds dealing high damage to anyone remaining in the circle.

subjectDetailCards.title: Noru'usk, Servant of Oryx
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Taken Cabal
subjectDetailCards.description: Noru'usk has no special ability and behaves identically to encountering him in the Prison of Elders: Taken.

headerSections.textBlock:
Updated after the events of The Taken King, this version of Prison of Elders features updated rewards and new possible Taken versions featuring rounds of Taken enemies and 4 new Taken bosses.

Like the original, this will rotate through 5 rounds of 3 waves, randomly activating a modifier for the round. Each round takes place in one of the four cell blocks, corresponding with a specific enemy type. Rounds 2-4 will begin to add a Critical Objective to the 2nd and 3rd wave, which must be completed or the entire round will be failed and restarted. The final round consists of a boss fight randomly selected from a list of options. Complete this final round, then descend below the central airlock room to claim your rewards.

Notable rewards are mostly the House of Judgment Reputation gained, and the option to gain an otherwise deprecated Sterling Treasure on the end screen Activity Rewards. However the drop limit for this is shared with the Treasure of Ages from the Weekly Story Playlist.

sections.textBlock: Potential rewards listed presume that you open all 3 chests.

summaryRewards.rewardText: Sterling Treasure (Account Weekly Limit: 1 | Shared with Weekly Story Treasure)
Legendary Marks +10 (Account Weekly Limit: 3 | Shared with SIVA Crisis Heroic)

headerSections.textBlock:
The original Arena mode, unlocked after completing the House of Wolves quest. Prison of Elders will rotate through 5 rounds of 3 waves each, randomly including a modifier for the round. Each round takes place in one of the four cell blocks, corresponding with a specific enemy type. Rounds 2-4 will begin to add a Critical Objective to the 2nd and 3rd wave, which must be completed or the entire round will be failed and restarted. The final round consists of a mini-boss fight randomly selected from a list of options. Complete this final round, then descend below the central airlock room to claim your rewards.

Notable rewards include a guaranteed random Year 1 Exotic for the first time clear of any character, as well as the potential for otherwise deprecated Legacy Legendary Engrams from the House of Wolves era. Otherwise, rewards are House of Judgment Reputation, escalating amounts of experience, Year 1 Prison of Elders gear, and a reliable source of Strange Coins.

sections.textBlock: Potential rewards listed presume that you open all 3 chests; the large chest no longer requires a Treasure Key to open, and can be opened every run.

summaryRewards.rewardText: 1-2 Legacy Legendary Prison of Elders Items
(Possible) Token of Judgment, Identity, or Flight

sections.textBlock: Drop rates for this gear on the Activity Rewards screen are low enough to prohibit adequate data gathering. This list is provided from a combination of limited anecdotal reporting and Year 1 items tagged with a Prison of Elders source in the API. There is a non-zero chance that items included here cannot drop, or that items not included here (such as *House of Wolves* era Faction armor) can.

headerSections.textBlock:
One of two Prison of Elders challenge nodes, which runs a pre-constructed Prison of Elders instance with a unique boss as the final round. Current challenge changes at weekly reset. This version is focused on gaining armor, awarding an Armor Core previously used to purchase armor from Variks, now the large chest can directly award armor. Small chest and end screen rewards appear to be consistent with the Level 28 version of Prison of Elders.

Certain rewards are on a weekly lockout, possibly including the experience rewards.

sections.textBlock: Potential rewards listed presume that you open all 3 chests; the large chest no longer requires a Treasure Key to open, and can be opened every run.

summaryRewards.rewardText: 1-2 Legacy Legendary Prison of Elders Items
(Possible) Token of Judgment, Identity, or Flight

activityCards.title: Broken Legion
activityCards.description: Valus Trau'ug seeks to prove his might by crushing all who challenge him.

activityCards.title: Cult of the Worm
activityCards.description: Gulrot, Unclean has been summoned by the Worm Keepers. Don't let the beast's filth spread beyond the prison.

activityCards.title: Machine Wrath
activityCards.description: Archon Servants have raised their own corrupted Servitor-god, Kaliks Reborn. Defeat it and be rewarded.

activityCards.title: The Forever Eater
activityCards.description: Qodron, a Vex chronovore, sees only one outcome: your demise. Prove it wrong.

activityCards.title: Urrox’s Grudge
activityCards.description: The Flame Prince Urrox vows to burn the Light until all that remains is Darkness and bone.

headerSections.textBlock:
One of two Prison of Elders challenge nodes, which runs a pre-constructed Prison of Elders instance with a unique boss as the final round. Current challenge changes at weekly reset. This version is focused on gaining armor, awarding a Weapon Core previously used to purchase weapons from Variks, now the large chest can directly award weapons. Small chest and end screen rewards appear to be consistent with the Level 28 version of Prison of Elders.

Certain rewards are on a weekly lockout, possibly including the experience rewards.

sections.textBlock: Potential rewards listed presume that you open all 3 chests; the large chest no longer requires a Treasure Key to open, and can be opened every run.

summaryRewards.rewardText: 1-2 Legacy Legendary Prison of Elders Items
(Possible) Token of Judgment, Identity, or Flight

activityCards.title: Broken Legion
activityCards.description: Valus Trau'ug seeks to prove his might by crushing all who challenge him.

activityCards.title: Cult of the Worm
activityCards.description: Gulrot, Unclean has been summoned by the Worm Keepers. Don't let the beast's filth spread beyond the prison.

activityCards.title: Machine Wrath
activityCards.description: Archon Servants have raised their own corrupted Servitor-god, Kaliks Reborn. Defeat it and be rewarded.

activityCards.title: The Forever Eater
activityCards.description: Qodron, a Vex chronovore, sees only one outcome: your demise. Prove it wrong.

activityCards.title: Urrox’s Grudge
activityCards.description: The Flame Prince Urrox vows to burn the Light until all that remains is Darkness and bone.

headerSections.textBlock:
This challenge is available all weeks, and served as the alternative to a new raid in House of Wolves. Like the other challenges this is another pre-constructed instance, however the fifth round is the Pilot Servitor mini-boss, and this is followed by a sixth and final round fighting Skolas.

Certain rewards are on a weekly lockout, possibly including the experience rewards.

sections.textBlock: Immediately after killing Skolas for the first time per character per week, there is a chance to drop one of these variants of the primary Prison of Elders primary weapons with random elemental damage.
Potential rewards listed presume that you open all 3 chests; the large chest no longer requires a Treasure Key to open, and can be opened every run.

summaryRewards.rewardText: 1-2 Legacy Legendary Prison of Elders Items
(Possible) Token of Judgment, Identity, or Flight

subjectDetailCards.title: Round 1
subjectDetailCards.description:
Enemy: Hive

Modifier: Exposure + Brawler

Objective: n/a

subjectDetailCards.title: Round 2
subjectDetailCards.description:
Enemy: Vex

Modifier: Grounded + Airborne

Objective: Destroy Mines

subjectDetailCards.title: Round 3
subjectDetailCards.description:
Enemy: Cabal

Modifier: Catapult + Arc Burn

Objective: Dismantle Mines

subjectDetailCards.title: Round 4
subjectDetailCards.description:
Enemy: Fallen

Modifier: Specialist + Juggler

Objective: Kill Target

subjectDetailCards.title: Pilot Servitor
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Mini Boss
subjectDetailCards.description:
Enemy: Fallen

Modifier: Trickle + Small Arms

subjectDetailCards.title: Skolas, Kell of Kells
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Boss
subjectDetailCards.description:
Enemy: Fallen

Modifier changes weekly:

Brawler + Juggler
Small Arms + Lightswitch
Catapult + Angry

headerSections.textBlock:
While on Patrol in any destination, locations may contain blinking patrol beacons which will offer a mission when interacted with. Pull out your Ghost to enter Nav Mode and see icons for each available patrol mission to determine their type. You can also abandon a patrol mission at any time if you want to, by pulling out your Ghost and holding the Abandon Objective input indicated.

Patrol missions primarily reward Vanguard Reputation, in different amounts based on mission type. Secret Patrol Missions additionally reward some Strange Coins.

subjectDetailCards.title: High Value target
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: VIP
subjectDetailCards.description: Follow your waypoint and eliminate the VIP target.

subjectDetailCards.title: Kill Any Enemies
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Kill
subjectDetailCards.description: Eliminate a specific amount of enemies, any types will do.

subjectDetailCards.title: Kill & Collect
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Collect
subjectDetailCards.description: Eliminate specified enemies, and collect dropped items.

subjectDetailCards.title: Gather Data
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Scan
subjectDetailCards.description: Follow your waypoint and have your Ghost scan the target.

subjectDetailCards.title: Scout an Area
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Survey
subjectDetailCards.description: Follow your waypoint and remain at the location to gather data.

subjectDetailCards.title: Collect Energy Spikes
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Race
subjectDetailCards.description: Pass through energy spikes in sequence before time runs out.

subjectDetailCards.title: Eliminate Taken Force
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Taken
subjectDetailCards.description: Follow your waypoint, then eliminate the Taken forces you find. Kill all of the Taken quickly enough to draw out their leader.

subjectDetailCards.title: Quarantine
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Splicer
subjectDetailCards.description: Kill the Machine Priest to disrupt a Devil Splicer ritual, then defend your position against the responding attackers.

activityCards.title: Patrol the Cosmodrome
activityCards.subtitle: Old Russia, Earth
activityCards.description: Scour the Old Russian launch port and aid the City in their exploration efforts.

activityCards.title: Patrol the Moon
activityCards.subtitle: Ocean of Storms, Moon
activityCards.description: Survive the shattered surface of Earth's Moon and aid the City in their efforts.

activityCards.title: Patrol Venus
activityCards.subtitle: Ishtar Sink, Venus
activityCards.description: Keep watch in the Ishtar Sink to aid the City in its efforts.

activityCards.title: Patrol Mars
activityCards.subtitle: Meridian Bay, Mars
activityCards.description: Fight through the shifting Martian dunes and aid the City in their exploration efforts.

activityCards.title: Patrol the Dreadnaught
activityCards.subtitle: Dreadnaught, Rings of Saturn
activityCards.description: Board the Taken King's Dreadnaught. Prove the Vanguard's dominance over the Hive and Taken.

activityCards.title: Patrol the Plaguelands
activityCards.subtitle: Old Russia, Earth
activityCards.description: Explore beyond the Cosmodrome's ancient walls.

headerSections.textBlock: While on Patrol a Public Event may become active in your current location. All Guardians present may participate, and will receive rewards immediately upon successful completion of the objective. Optimal objective completion will earn Gold Tier rewards, while objective failure will award nothing. The first Gold Tier completion of the day provides additional rewards.

sections.textBlock: The Dreadnaught and Plaguelands each include a special activity classified as a type of Public Event, but unique to a specific location and directly activated by players using consumables. Each of these has it’s own materials to activate, unique encounters and rewards. See their individual activity pages for details.

activityCards.title: Court of Oryx
activityCards.subtitle: Dreadnaught, Rings of Saturn
activityCards.description: In the Hall of Souls aboard the Dreadnaught, a gateway to the Ascendant Plane awaits. Present Summoning Runes to the altars to call forth champions of Oryx’s court.

activityCards.title: Archon’s Forge
activityCards.subtitle: Plaguelands, Old Russia, Earth
activityCards.description: Inside Archon’s Keep, the Splicers have twisted a ritual of honor into a proving ground for augmentation. Present SIVA offerings to incite the Devils to slaughter.

subjectDetailCards.title: Defeat Extraction Crews
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Stop fallen from extracting Glimmer
subjectDetailCards.description: The Fallen will send out three mining crews on Skiffs. Defeat each crew in sequence before time runs out to destroy their drill and disrupt their Glimmer mining operation.

subjectDetailCards.title: Destroy Fallen Walker
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Take out the Drill-Guarding Walker
subjectDetailCards.description: The Fallen have sent out a Walker to protect their Glimmer Drill. Destroy the Walker before time runs out to disrupt their Glimmer mining operations.

subjectDetailCards.title: Defend the Warsat
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Stay near to prepare transmat
subjectDetailCards.description: A satellite from the Warmind network has crashed on the surface. Defend it from our enemies while your Ghost prepares to transmat the WARSAT away.

subjectDetailCards.title: Eliminate the Target
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Before it Reaches the Destination
subjectDetailCards.description: Intel reports that a powerful enemy is passing through this area. Kill this high priority target before it can travel along it’s planned route and escape.

subjectDetailCards.title: Prevent Vex Sacrifices
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Keep Vex away from the Conflux
subjectDetailCards.description: The Vex are attempting to sacrifice themselves to a nearby conflux. Who knows why, but it can’t be good. Kill the Vex to keep them away from the conflux.

sections.textBlock:
Public Events have three possible reward outcomes, which are fairly simple. To achieve Gold Tier rewards, complete the objective before time runs out. Silver Tier is awarded if time runs out, but the objective was nearly completed. Bronze Tier is awarded when time runs out, and the objective has not been sufficiently progressed.

Prevent Vex Sacrifices is an exception, requiring you to defend the conflux for the duration with the difficulty of enemies gradually increasing. Gold Tier is awarded if no sacrifices are allowed, Silver Tier is awarded if even 1 Vex manages to successfully sacrifice itself, and Bronze Tier is awarded if 6 or more Vex sacrifice. If 10 sacrifices are allowed, the event is failed with no reward.

Occasionally in certain locations instead of a Public Event, you may experience a different kind of interruption. These do not function or count as Public Events, do not feature a displayed timer, and do not structure rewards into the above tiers.

The most prevalent and relevant of these are incursions of **“Taken forces are corrupting the area.”** This type of event is necessary to complete certain Queen’s Wrath bounties to hunt Taken Lieutenants and WANTED Taken targets. Killing all 3 Taken Lieutenants will spawn a zealot boss reinforced by more Taken. Killing the boss before the hidden timer runs out will drop a Guaranteed Reciprocal Rune in addition to the standard potential engram drops of an Ultra. See the Queen’s Wrath bounty information for location and boss details.

Additionally you may experience messages such as **“The enemy is moving against each other,”** in which case waves of two enemy factions will continuously spawn to fight one another for a short time, including many Major enemies. Or you may see **“A roving band of pikes is moving this way,”** shortly followed by a trio of Dregs riding Pikes which you can steal by killing the riders.

In the Rubicon Wastes on Mars, you may encounter the message **“A pack of Wolves is prowling,”** after which a group of Level 38 Fallen enemies will spawn on the edge of the map. If killed, the Wolves will become frenzied and a Skiff will drop multiple waves of enemies. If all of these are killed *Keldar, Archon Priest* will spawn as the rest of the Wolves retreat. Killing him provides no special rewards, but he is an Ultra and thus has the chance to drop a Legendary Engram or trigger the effect of Three of Coins. This event is notable as it is the only surviving example of a regular feature to hunt WANTED Fallen targets in *House of Wolves*.

headerSections.textBlock:
Located in the Hall of Souls aboard the Dreadnaught is a gate to the Ascendant Plane. Present Summoning Runes you’ve acquired to the Thrall-like altars here, and you will call forth champions of Oryx to challenge you. Adapt to any unique boss mechanics and defeat the summoned challenger before time runs out to complete the event. If the summoner is dead or absent from the arena for more than 20 seconds, the event will fail. Upon failure for any reason the Summoning Rune will be refunded. Higher quality runes bring greater challenges and greater reward potential.

Only the summoner is guaranteed a reward. After event completion there is a 40 second cooldown applied to allow other Guardians the chance to summon.

subjectDetailCards.title: Reciprocal Rune
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Summoning Rune
subjectDetailCards.description: An ancient relic that summons one of Oryx's lieutenants in the Court of Oryx.

subjectDetailCards.title: Stolen Rune
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Summoning Rune
subjectDetailCards.description:
A rune that grows in power as you summon and banish enemies with Reciprocal Runes in the Court of Oryx.

Once charged, it can summon a pair of Oryx's lieutenants in the Court of Oryx.

subjectDetailCards.title: Antiquated Rune
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Summoning Rune
subjectDetailCards.description:
A rune that grows in power as you summon and banish enemies with Stolen Runes in the Court of Oryx.

Once charged, it can be used to summon one of Oryx's most powerful lieutenants in the Court of Oryx.

sections.textBlock: Successfully defeating the challenger for an Antiquated Rune will reward the summoner with a Legendary Artifact for their class, from the Crota’s Bane set. This Artifact will drop with a maximum power of 320, but can be infused to the cap of 400. This set of Artifacts is characterized by even stat roll chances along with fixed elemental Catalyst perks; each Artifact enables the chance for orb generation on precision kills with a certain element. As such, these are best paired with Sidearms, Sniper Rifles, Machine Guns, or the reprised raid Exotic Adept Primary Weapons.
Offering a Reciprocal Rune will randomly summon one of the following challengers at random. Defeat them before time runs out, and do not let the summoner be absent from the arena.

subjectDetailCards.title: Alzok Däl, Gornuk Däl, Zyrok Däl
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Hive Wizard Trio
subjectDetailCards.description:
These three Hive sisters have cut away their deaths and hidden them within one another.

If even one remains alive, the others will soon revive.

Act quickly to kill all three in short succession so they can remain dead, together.

subjectDetailCards.title: Bracus Horu'usk
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Taken Centurion
subjectDetailCards.description:
Once a Centurion of the Cabal Empire, Horu'usk has been Taken. Now his might is the loyalty he commands.

While supported by his forces, he is invulnerable. Eliminate his minions to peel his defenses, until he summons reinforcements.

subjectDetailCards.title: Mengoor and Cra'adug
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Cursed Knight Duo
subjectDetailCards.description:
As punishment Oryx took one as lesson and left the other as learner, then cursed them: “Let separation rule you. Let companionship be your ruin.”

Draw them close together to expose their weakness.

subjectDetailCards.title: Krughor
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Ogre of the Witch Queen
subjectDetailCards.description:
A creation of the Witch Queen, Savathûn, this Ogre's death is hidden inside another curse.

Detonate the Cursed Thrall it summons forth while they are near to the Ogre to strip away its invincible state.

subjectDetailCards.title: Vorlog
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Named, “Lesser than me”
subjectDetailCards.description:
Vorlog is a master of both armaments and Hive magic. If their shield is broken they will soon regenerate it in a different element.

Overwhelm this Knight with continued firepower to cut short their violent potential.

subjectDetailCards.title: Lokaar
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: named, “Not there”
subjectDetailCards.description:
After failing to assassinate Omnigul, this Wizard’s unique teleportation saw her lifted to Oryx’s court.

Track of her within the arena and you will soon eliminate this evasive nuisance.

sections.textBlock: Offering a Stolen Rune will randomly summon one of eight possible pairs of Tier 1 bosses. Defeating specific pairing will additionally award Calcified Fragments 39-42 Defeat them before time runs out, and do not let the summoner be absent from the arena.

subjectDetailCards.title: Calcified Fragment XXXIX
subjectDetailCards.description:
**Drops from the following Tier 2 combinations:**

Lokaar + Vorlog

Lokaar + Mengoor and Cra'adug

subjectDetailCards.title: Calcified Fragment XL
subjectDetailCards.description:
**Drops from the following Tier 2 combinations:**

Bracus Horu'usk +  Mengoor and Cra'adug

Bracus Horu'usk + Alzok Däl, Gornuk Däl, Zyrok Däl

subjectDetailCards.title: Calcified Fragment XLI
subjectDetailCards.description:
**Drops from the following Tier 2 combinations:**

Bracus Horu'usk + Vorlog

Krughor + Mengoor and Cra'adug

subjectDetailCards.title: Calcified Fragment XLII
subjectDetailCards.description:
**Drops from the following Tier 2 combinations:**

Krughor + Alzok Däl, Gornuk Däl, Zyrok Däl

Krughor + Vorlog

sections.textBlock: Offering an Antiquated Rune will summon one of the most powerful champions of the court, changing each week. Defeat them before time runs out, and do not let the summoner be absent from the arena. Each of these bosses also awards a Calcified Fragment the first time you defeat them, unlocking Fragments 47-49.

subjectDetailCards.title: Thalnok, Fanatic of Crota
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Faithful, Foolish Proxy
subjectDetailCards.description:
After Crota’s exile, Thalnok imitated the banished prince and amused Oryx. So true is he as proxy, you must slay him as you slew Crota.

Kill his swordbearer, *Hagrist, Blade’s Edge*. Destroy Thalnok’s protective shield, and turn the blade against the pretender.

subjectDetailCards.title: Balwûr
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Daughter of Savathûn
subjectDetailCards.description:
This vile Wizard floods the arena with a poison that brings harm and wards her against the poisoned. A pool of Light can cleanse this poison.

Slay her Acolytes to free the Light they’ve consumed, creating more temporary pools to fight back from.

subjectDetailCards.title: Kagoor
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: Taken Master of Ogres
subjectDetailCards.description:
Accompanied by her Servile Ogre, Kagoor is invulnerable.

Destroy her Maleficent Eyes to be empowered against her Ogres. Eliminate the Servile Ogre, and slay her quickly before she summons another to her defense.

headerSections.textBlock:
Located within Archon’s Keep is a ritual ground for the Devil Splicers. Present SIVA Offerings to the forge to incite the Devils to violence, then slaughter them. The SIVA Offering used will alter the type of enemies that respond, higher quality Offerings will call more challenging opponents and offer a higher drop rate for rewards. If you die and respawn yourself, or arrive to an ongoing event, you will need to use a Splicer Key to open and enter the arena.

To increase the chance of matching with other players it is recommended to move through the halls approaching the Forge slowly to provide time to matchmake.

subjectDetailCards.title: Fused SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a Vandal Shank Master fused with basic SIVA technology.

subjectDetailCards.title: Fused SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a trio of dregs fused with basic SIVA technology.

subjectDetailCards.title: Fused SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a Captain fused with basic SIVA technology.

subjectDetailCards.title: Fused SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a Vandal fused with basic SIVA technology.

subjectDetailCards.title: Fused SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a Servitor fused with basic SIVA technology.

subjectDetailCards.title: Enhanced SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a ruthless dreg enhanced with SIVA augmentations.

subjectDetailCards.title: Enhanced SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a Shank Runner enhanced with SIVA augmentations.

subjectDetailCards.title: Enhanced SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face an onslaught of Splicers led by a crew of Vandals enhanced with SIVA augmentations.

subjectDetailCards.title: Enhanced SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a pair of Captains enhanced with SIVA augmentations.

subjectDetailCards.title: Enhanced SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a pair of Servitors enhanced with SIVA augmentations.

subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a trio of shanks perfected through SIVA experimentations.

subjectDetailCards.title: Perfected SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a dreg perfected through SIVA experimentations.

subjectDetailCards.title: Perfected SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a Captain perfected through SIVA experimentations.

subjectDetailCards.title: Perfected SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a Vandal perfected through SIVA experimentations.

subjectDetailCards.title: Perfected SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.subtitle: SIVA Offering
subjectDetailCards.description:
Used to incite the fury of the Devil Splicers in Archon's Forge.

Face a Splicer onslaught led by a Servitor perfected through SIVA experimentations.

headerSections.textBlock:
This node rotates through five lists of story missions, changing each weekly reset. Launching this activity will randomly start one of the included story missions, with modifiers added to increase difficulty and variety. Upon completion of the mission you will have to return to orbit and reselect the node to launch another mission.

The primary rewards are a single weekly Treasure of Ages, a weekly bonus of Legendary Marks that can be earned 5 times, and a reward of Legendary Marks for the first daily completion. Additionally you will always receive a small amount of random Destination Materials (not related to which playlist is in rotation) and experience.

summaryRewards.rewardText: Treasure of Ages (Account Weekly Limit: 1 | Shared with Prison of Elders: Taken Sterling Treasure)

activityCards.title: Earth and Moon
activityCards.subtitle: A Guardian Rises
activityCards.description: Every legend has a beginning…

activityCards.title: Mars and Venus
activityCards.subtitle: A Stranger’s Call
activityCards.description: When Destiny called, you answered.

activityCards.title: Dark Champions
activityCards.subtitle: The Dark Below / House of Wolves
activityCards.description: The Eater of Hope and a would-be Kell of Kells challenge the safety of humanity.

activityCards.title: The Taken War
activityCards.subtitle: The Taken King
activityCards.description: Oryx seeks vengeance for his lost son.

activityCards.title: The Iron Lords
activityCards.subtitle: Rise of Iron
activityCards.description: The Iron Lords are gone. It is up to you to finish their final mission.

headerSections.textBlock: Each week the Featured Crucible Mode node will shift through a fixed rotation of modes, presented in order below. First completion of a match in this node per week will award a Treasure of Ages, and all match completions will award 10 Legendary Marks. Additional rewards may be obtained from the end of match Activity Rewards screen, which shares a standardized Crucible Activity Rewards loot table. These additional rewards are not correlated to performance, they only require you to complete the match and not leave prematurely.

summaryRewards.rewardText: Crucible Reputation +120 for win, +90 for loss

subjectDetailCards.title: Mayhem Rumble
subjectDetailCards.description: Embrace the chaos. Greatly increased ability recharge rates. Faster Special Weapon and Heavy Ammo spawns. No respawn delay. Scoring values multiplied by 10.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "How will you handle a battle like this, I wonder?" —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Combined Arms
subjectDetailCards.description: All-out team warfare with vehicles. Destroy the enemy.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "Crush your enemies with a hundred tons of plated hull and munitions fire." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Zone Control
subjectDetailCards.description: Capture zones and hold the zones to win.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "It's a tactical exercise in reinforcing and defending acquired territory. Should the Vanguard ever decide to expand past the walls of the City, the Guardians will be ready." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Mayhem Clash
subjectDetailCards.description: Embrace the chaos. Greatly increased ability recharge rates. Faster Special and Heavy Ammo spawns. No respawn delay. Scoring values multiplied by 10.

subjectDetailCards.title: Doubles
subjectDetailCards.description: Small-team tactics. Kill the enemy. Revive teammates.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "Leave no ally behind, and no enemy standing." —Lord Shaxx

headerSections.textBlock:
Crucible unlocks at character level 10. The standard Crucible nodes offer 5 modes, with most nodes alternating between 2 possible modes each week. The center node will always be Elimination, as it’s alternate mode was Trials of Osiris which is no longer scheduled. Lord Shaxx always offers weekly bounties for both available 6V6 modes.

All modes will award 15 Legendary Marks. Additional rewards may be obtained from the end of match Activity Rewards screen, which shares a standardized Crucible Activity Rewards loot table. These additional rewards are not correlated to performance, they only require you to complete the match and not leave prematurely.

summaryRewards.rewardText: Crucible Reputation +120 for win, +90 for loss

subjectDetailCards.title: Control
subjectDetailCards.description: Capture zones to increase points for every kill.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "It's a tactical exercise in reinforcing and defending acquired territory. Should the Vanguard ever decide to expand past the walls of the City, the Guardians will be ready." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Clash
subjectDetailCards.description: All-out team warfare. Destroy the enemy.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "No mercy. No hesitation. It'll take nothing less to defeat the Darkness." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Supremacy
subjectDetailCards.description: Defeat your opponents, and take their Crests.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "A Guardian's Crest is her honor. Claim them from opponents, and protect your own." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Rift
subjectDetailCards.description: Collect the Spark and ignite the enemy's Rift with it.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "This all started as a Warlock focus drill. The things I've seen them do with Rifts would be...very useful on a battlefield." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Elimination
subjectDetailCards.description: Small-team tactics. Revive teammates to keep fighting. Bring down the entire opposing team to claim victory.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "Your allies are your lifeblood. No second chances except the ones you give." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Salvage
subjectDetailCards.description: Race the other team to capture and hold relics.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "Treasures and forgotten tech lie waiting in the wake of the Golden Age's collapse. One must be swift and fierce to claim them." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Skirmish
subjectDetailCards.description: Small-team tactics. Kill the enemy. Revive teammates.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "Leave no ally behind, and no enemy standing." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Rumble
subjectDetailCards.description: Kill everything. Watch your back.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "Pure combat. Your only job is to make sure you're still standing when the dust settles." —Lord Shaxx

subjectDetailCards.title: Rumble Supremacy
subjectDetailCards.description: Battle on your own. Defeat your opponents, and take their Crests.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "A Guardian's Crest is her honor. Claim them from opponents, and protect your own." —Lord Shaxx
